images.caption: Figure 1:Overview of the HyPER-GAN method, which includes four phases: a) datasets and preprocessing, b) real-world dataset indexing, c) training, and d) photorealism enhancement.
Figure 2:Examples of matched patches between the generated (top) and the real-world (bottom) patches.
Figure 3:Translation results of PFD (GTA-V) towards real-world datasets (CS and MV) produced by a) EPE and b) HyPER-GAN.
Figure 4:Translation results of PFD (GTA-V) towards the real-world datasets CS and MV produced by a) FastCUT, b) REGEN, and c) HyPER-GAN.

header: Appendix AComparison with a Diffusion Model

images.caption: Figure 5:Comparison of images generated by (b) COSMOS Transfer1 and (c) HyPER-GAN, given as input an image (a) from the PFB dataset.

header: Appendix BCross-Engine Evaluation on a Simulator

images.caption: Figure 6:Photorealism-enhanced image (right) produced by HyPER-GAN given a synthetic image from the CARLA (Unreal Engine 5) simulator (left).
